updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.160 — Security Prompts and Config File Protection
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.160 ships two significant security improvements: it now prompts before writing to shell startup files (.zshenv, .zlogin, .bash_login, ~/.config/git/) and before modifying build-tool config files that grant code execution (.npmrc, .yarnrc*, bunfig.toml, .bazelrc, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .devcontainer/). Edit no longer requires a separate Read step after a grep command, reducing friction for single-file edits. Clipboard handling on Windows/WSL is fixed to use PowerShell interop instead of OSC 52, and the dynamic-workflow trigger keyword is renamed from 'workflow' to 'ultracode'. The deprecated CLAUDE_CODE_OPUS_4_6_FAST_MODE_OVERRIDE environment variable is removed.
updates.accuracy_notes: Directly sourced from Anthropic's official GitHub releases page for claude-code. Version, timestamp, and all listed changes are verifiable. The 'ultracode' rename and security prompts are notable behavioral changes users should be aware of.

Slide 4: Fix: Clipboard now works correctly in Windows WSL
Slide 5: Heads up: 'workflow' keyword renamed to 'ultracode'

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.161 — OTEL Metrics, MCP Connectors, and Parallel Tool Call Fixes
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.161 adds support for OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES values as labels on metric datapoints, enabling teams to slice usage metrics by custom dimensions like team name or repo. The agent UI now shows 'done/total' progress counts when work fans out across multiple agents, with peek showing the longest-running item. The /mcp command now collapses unused claude.ai connectors behind a 'Show unused connectors' toggle to reduce clutter. Critically, failed Bash commands in parallel tool calls no longer cancel sibling calls in the same batch — each tool now returns results independently. Linux clipboard is upgraded to wl-copy/xclip/xsel with both clipboard and PRIMARY selection support for middle-click paste.
updates.accuracy_notes: Directly sourced from Anthropic's official GitHub releases page. The parallel tool call fix is a notable behavioral change that affects agentic workflows. All features are verifiable against the changelog.

updates.title: Anthropic's Claude is down... again
updates.summary: Claude AI experienced a major service disruption on June 2, 2026, starting at approximately 2:10am ET (06:04 UTC), with elevated error rates across Claude.ai, Claude Console, Claude API, and Claude Code. Anthropic's status page confirmed the incident, with a fix implemented by 11:49 UTC. The root cause was a critical bug in Claude Code's sub-agent system that caused agents to multiply exponentially and run in an infinite loop, cascading into broader service degradation. The outage came a day after Anthropic filed confidentially for an IPO, drawing attention to reliability questions at a pivotal moment for the company.
updates.accuracy_notes: Article identified via search indexing; direct page access blocked (HTTP 403). Publication date is inferred from strong contextual alignment with June 2 outage coverage across TechRadar, The National News, Sportskeeda, ibtimes.com.au, and status.claude.com incident records. Outage timeline and root cause (sub-agent infinite loop) corroborated by multiple independent outlets. The IPO timing context is confirmed via CBS News (June 1, 2026 confidential IPO filing).
